latest.title: EBT Share Purchase
latest.content:
1 Jun 2026 12:05
RNS Number : 4910G
Frontier Developments PLC
01 June 2026
1 June 2026
Frontier Developments plc
EBT Share Purchase
Frontier Developments plc (AIM: FDEV, 'Frontier', the 'Group' or the 'Company'), a leading developer and publisher of video games based in Cambridge, UK, was notified on 29 May 2026, by Ocorian Limited, the trustee of the Frontier Developments plc Employee Benefit Trust (the 'EBT'), that the EBT had purchased 230,000 ordinary shares of 0.5p each in the Company ('Ordinary Shares') at an average price of 418 pence per Ordinary Share on 29 May 2026.
These Ordinary Shares are to be held in the EBT and are intended to be used to satisfy the exercise of share options by employees. The EBT is a discretionary trust for the benefit of the Company's employees, including the Directors of the Company. The purchase of Ordinary Shares by the EBT has been funded from the Company's existing cash resources.
Following this transaction, a total of 1,225,986 Ordinary Shares are held by the EBT, representing approximately 3.45% of the Company's total voting rights.
Enquiries:
Sign up for email alerts at
www.frontier.co.uk
/investors
Frontier Developments
IR@frontier.co.uk
+44 (0)1223 394300
Peel Hunt - Nomad and Corporate Broker
Neil Patel / Ben Cryer / Kate Bannatyne
+44 (0)20 7418 8900
About Frontier Developments plc
Frontier Developments plc is a leading independent developer and publisher of video games, specialising in creative management simulation (CMS) experiences. Headquartered in Cambridge, UK, Frontier develops and nurtures globally successful game franchises, including core CMS franchises such as
Planet Coaster
,
Planet Zoo
, and
Jurassic World Evolution
, using its proprietary COBRA technology. The CMS genre underpins Frontier's strategy, which is driving long-term and sustainable growth through strong player engagement, robust back-catalogue performance, and an exciting pipeline of new games.
Frontier's LEI number: 213800B9LGPWUAZ9GX18.

latest.title: Planet Zoo 2 Announced
latest.content:
28 May 2026 14:30
RNS Number : 9328F
Frontier Developments PLC
28 May 2026
BUILD A WILDER WORLD THIS OCTOBER IN PLANET ZOO 2 WITH AQUATIC AND FLYING SPECIES
Frontier's iconic animal management simulation returns with customisable wildlife reserves and cosy creativity at its core
Frontier Developments plc (AIM: FDEV) are excited to announce
Planet Zoo 2
. Building upon the critically acclaimed and beloved
Planet Zoo, this sequel
features both aquatic and flying species, alongside a deeper conservation focus.
Planet Zoo 2
will launch on PC, PlayStation®5, and Xbox Series X|S on 13 October 2026.
Let creativity soar in Frontier's most advanced zoo simulation game to date.
Planet Zoo 2
introduces new awe-inspiring animals, plus, for the first time aviaries and aquariums! Zookeepers are tasked with constructing beautiful habitats where authentic species can fully interact with their environments. What's more, players can now take conservation outside of their zoos, restoring natural habitats and releasing animals into Wildlife Reserves.
Planet Zoo 2
represents a generational leap forward in animation and animal behaviour.
Watch the Planet Zoo 2 Announce trailer HERE
From high-fidelity fur, feathers, and scales through to authentic behaviour and brachiation, in
Planet Zoo 2
, players will encounter a wide range of animals, each with their own needs. Meeting these needs will require Zookeepers to craft habitats that mirror species' natural environments, creating an authentic home for the animals and an amazing experience for the guests.
A new world beneath the water awaits Zookeepers with fully aquatic species making their debut with a splash. Black-tipped Reef Sharks, Hawksbill Turtles, and colourful shoals of fish are just a handful of the fresh and saltwater animals joining
Planet Zoo 2
. Notably, aquariums are not simply placeable tanks but fully customisable habitats that grant players control over scenery, location, and more. Plus, Zookeepers will have to ensure their aquariums are kept in top condition with filtration and an airlock for staff to enter.
Frequent flyers also enter the fold with aviaries featuring a mix of bright and brilliant fully flying birds, including the vibrant Toco Toucan and majestic Secretary Bird. Like aquariums, aviaries are fully editable, with players able to place scenery as they please. Aviaries introduce support poles which allow players to generate habitat covers at the click of a button. Plus, Zoo guests can even walk through suitable aviaries, creating opportunities for guest entertainment.
Conservation in
Planet Zoo 2
goes further than ever before. Zookeepers will take on conservation projects across the globe, revitalising expansive Wildlife Reserves and creating living ecosystems by releasing animals into their natural habitats.
From ensuring animals are kept in habitats that mirror their natural environments to creating immersive spaces for zoo guests to interact and learn,
Planet Zoo 2
offers players a meaningful management experience. From managing staff morale and training to constructing the perfect habitat, it's vital for budding Zookeepers to balance guest satisfaction with animal wellbeing in order to boost their rating and ensure their zoo becomes a roaring success as it grows.
From today, players who pre-order will receive the Bonus Content Pack at launch, which includes the Toucan Eat Shop and Signage, three Unique Animal Donation Bins, and the Tiger Photo Stand-In Wall.
Dedicated Zookeepers looking to expand their animal conservation efforts further can purchase the
Planet Zoo 2
: Deluxe Edition, which bundles the
Planet Zoo 2
base game alongside the
Planet Zoo 2
: Deluxe Upgrade Pack. This introduces an additional six animal species from across the globe, as well as a selection of exclusive animal signs to decorate their park.
Planet Zoo 2
will be available on PC, PlayStation®5, and Xbox Series X|S for a suggested retail price of £39.99/$49.99/€49.99 for the Standard Edition, and £54.99/$64.99/€64.99 for the Deluxe Edition.
Visit the
Planet Zoo 2
website or follow us on socials for more information:
www.planetzoogame.com
X (Formally Twitter)
Facebook
Instagram
YouTube
TikTok
Discord
About Frontier
Frontier Developments plc is a leading independent developer and publisher of video games, specialising in creative management simulation (CMS) experiences. Headquartered in Cambridge, UK, Frontier develops and nurtures globally successful game franchises, including core CMS franchises such as
Planet Coaster
,
Planet Zoo
, and
Jurassic World Evolution
, using its proprietary COBRA technology. The CMS genre underpins Frontier's strategy, which is driving long-term and sustainable growth through strong player engagement, robust back-catalogue performance, and an exciting pipeline of new games.

latest.title: Warhammer 40,000: Chaos Gate – Deathwatch Revealed
latest.content:
22 May 2026 11:26
RNS Number : 4870F
Frontier Developments PLC
22 May 2026
STAND VIGIL AGAINST YOUR FOE. WARHAMMER 40,000: CHAOS GATE - DEATHWATCH ANNOUNCED FOR PC AND CONSOLES
Suffer Not The Alien to Live. Lead the Imperium's sentinels with turn-based tactical precision in the sequel to the critically acclaimed Warhammer Chaos Gate - Daemonhunters
Frontier Developments plc (AIM: FDEV), and Complex Games, in collaboration with Warhammer are excited to announce
Warhammer 40,000: Chaos Gate
-
Deathwatch
. Sequel to the hugely popular
Warhammer 40,000: Chaos Gate - Daemonhunters
,
Deathwatch
will launch on PC, PlayStation®5, and Xbox Series X|S.
Lead the Long Vigil and take command of an elite Deathwatch Kill Team in the next brutal chapter of this critically acclaimed turn-based tactical RPG. Supported by agents of the Inquisition and Astra Militarum as newly sanctioned Inquisitors, commanders will wage war to drive back deadly Xenos threats and resist the corrupting forces of Chaos. In
Warhammer 40,000 Chaos Gate - Deathwatch
every decision matters. Each engagement will test players' discipline and strategy with broader class identity, new squad customisation, and challenging new missions across an epic campaign. Build the ultimate squad with a wide range of specialists from fanatical Inquisitorial agents to elite Deathwatch battle brothers, all of which introduce unique roles, abilities, and wargear. Plus, commanders seeking unmatched combat can trial their tactics in Skirmish mode, which offers fully customisable standalone battles for total control of the battlefield.
Watch the Warhammer Chaos Gate Deathwatch Announce trailer HERE
In the grim darkness of the far future, there is only war.
Warhammer 40,000: Chaos Gate
-
Deathwatch's
campaign showcases the brutality of Warhammer 40,000 through a narrative set on a galactic scale. Rising to power as Interrogator Bastian Rath, players are thrust into command amid a conspiracy threatening the stability of the Tyrian expanse. Across scorched planets, commanders will decide the fate of the sector and the Imperium's future within it as they uncover the truth left behind in the wake of Inquisitor Rykov.
For
Warhammer 40,000:
Chaos Gate - Deathwatch
combat has been redefined and the variety of allies, foes and battlefields expanded. During the campaign, commanders will face seven enemy factions as they build and lead a squad with up to nine playable classes. Recruit and customise the best of the best from across the Imperium and combine classes, weapons, abilities, and Space Marines from unique chapters to form the ultimate Kill Team, bringing devastation to those who would threaten the Imperium. Make use of the environment to crush foes, deliver brutal executions to continue the fight, or storm the battlefield with an array of towering vehicles including the Redemptor Dreadnought, Scout Sentinel, and Leman Russ tank.
Complex Games have worked closely with the experts at Warhammer to ensure authenticity at scale. The core of
Warhammer 40,000 Chaos Gate - Deathwatch
's development has been bringing the grim darkness of the 41st millennium to life. Armour, Chapter heraldry, wargear, insignias, and battle-scarred environments have been purposefully crafted to ensure they faithfully reflect the history and brutality of eternal war.
Suffer Not The Alien To Live - Wishlist and enlist for
Chaos Gate - Deathwatch
now.
Warhammer 40,000: Chaos Gate - Deathwatch
will be available on PC, PlayStation®5, and Xbox Series X|S.
Visit the
Warhammer 40,000: Chaos Gate - Deathwatch
website or follow us on socials for more information:
www.chaosgate.com
X (Formally Twitter)
Facebook
Instagram
YouTube
Discord
About Frontier
Frontier Developments plc is a leading independent developer and publisher of video games, specialising in creative management simulation (CMS) experiences. Headquartered in Cambridge, UK, Frontier develops and nurtures globally successful game franchises, including core CMS franchises such as
Planet Coaster
,
Planet Zoo
, and
Jurassic World Evolution
, using its proprietary COBRA technology. The CMS genre underpins Frontier's strategy, which is driving long-term and sustainable growth through strong player engagement, robust back-catalogue performance, and an exciting pipeline of new games.
About Games Workshop
Games Workshop Group PLC (LSE:GAW.L), based in Nottingham, UK, produces the best fantasy miniatures in the world. Games Workshop designs, manufactures, retails, and distributes its range of Warhammer Age of Sigmar and Warhammer 40,000 games, miniature soldiers, novels and model kits through more than 570 of its own stores (branded Games Workshop or Warhammer), its own web store, and independent retail channels in more than 50 countries worldwide. More information about Games Workshop and its other, related, brands and product ranges (including its publishing division 'Black Library' and its special resin miniatures studio 'Forge World') can be found at
www.games-workshop.com
.
About Complex Games
Complex Games is a video game studio based in Winnipeg, Canada, and a wholly owned subsidiary of Frontier Developments. The studio is best known for developing
Warhammer 40,000: Chaos Gate - Daemonhunters
, a turn-based tactical strategy game set in Games Workshop's iconic Warhammer 40,000 universe. As part of Frontier Developments, Complex Games focuses on creating deep, high-quality tactical gameplay experiences, combining innovative design principles with technical expertise.

company_30s.what_they_do: Frontier Developments is a British video‑game studio that builds large, open‑world space simulations and titles based on the Warhammer 40,000 franchise.  Its flagship games, like Elite Dangerous, draw millions of players who spend on expansions and micro‑transactions.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The studio owns two high‑profile IPs that generate strong community engagement and recurring revenue.  Its games have proven to keep players online for long periods, which is valuable for future monetisation and for attracting new fans through cross‑platform releases.
company_30s.current_state: The company has recently upgraded its FY26 revenue and profit outlook and completed a share‑buyback programme, signalling management confidence.  It is cash‑flow positive, reducing debt and giving it breathing room to fund new releases.  The share price, however, is still far below the level it reached before the last crash.

rns_narrative: Frontier announced a share‑buyback and a new Warhammer 40,000 title, showing it is still investing in growth while returning capital to shareholders.
The trading update confirms that revenue and profit are rising, suggesting the core business is stabilising after a period of volatility.
Recent share‑holding moves by directors and the completion of buyback transactions reinforce the narrative that the company is in a consolidation phase.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Fans are excited about the new Warhammer release but remain cautious because the stock has been highly volatile.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls point to the upgraded earnings forecast, the fresh IP launch, and the share‑buyback as signs that the business is on a solid footing.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears worry that a single new title could under‑perform and that development costs may stay high, keeping earnings growth uncertain.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment is still bullish but subdued; engagement is low because the stock has not yet shown a clear bounce.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat, meaning any upcoming news could catch the market by surprise.

near_term_catalysts: The release of Warhammer 40,000: Chaos Gate – Deathwatch in the next few weeks could lift sales and attract new players.
The June 2026 earnings report will test the upgraded guidance; a beat could trigger a rally.
If the company announces further capital return or a partnership with a major platform, that could add momentum.
A spike in trading volume that exceeds the 2‑month average could confirm that the market is starting to price in the upside.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals are a volume surge on the release day, a price break above the last 20‑day moving average, and a positive earnings surprise.  If any of these occur, the mean‑reversion thesis becomes more credible.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a recovery from a deep crash.  The APEX compression score indicates a tight range, so a breakout could happen in the next 2–3 months if the catalysts play out.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: A green‑colored signal historically delivers around a 40% average return, and the company’s recent earnings upgrade and new title launch give a clear catalyst for a rebound.  If the share climbs to the midpoint between its recent low and pre‑crash peak, the upside is roughly 50–100% from today’s level.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the new Warhammer game fails to meet expectations or if development costs rise sharply, the stock could stay flat or fall further.  The worst‑case scenario is that the price remains near its current trough.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Loss is capped at the current price; upside could be multiple times that if the mean reversion kicks in.  The asymmetry is favourable: a small downside risk versus a large potential upside.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a dip that confirms the 20‑day support level, size the position to 5–10% of your portfolio, and set a stop just below that support.  Use a trailing stop once the price moves 10% higher to protect gains.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the release day or earnings report.  Watch for volume >2× the 30‑day average and a price break above the 20‑day moving average.  Those are clear entry signals.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risk has been identified; the setup remains bullish.

ai_insights.bottom_line: After a deep crash, Frontier’s new Warhammer release and completed share‑buyback create a contrarian play with a high upside potential if the market starts to price in the mean reversion within the next few months.
